relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus approaches Jerusalem by Bethphage, Bethany, and the Mount of Olives.
The needed colt is already tied and waiting.
The Lord’s need releases it.
The disciples find the colt at the door.
Bystanders question the loosing.
Jesus’ word opens the way.
Garments become the seat for the humble entry.
The way is covered with garments and branches.
Hosanna rises around the one coming in the Lord’s name.
David’s coming reign is blessed.
Jesus enters, looks around the temple, and withdraws to Bethany.
Morning hunger begins the fig-tree sign.
Leaves without fruit draw his attention.
The fruitless tree becomes a sign heard by the disciples.
Jesus disrupts temple commerce.
He interrupts the temple as passageway for business.
The house of prayer for all nations has been distorted into a cave of predation.
The leaders fear him because the crowd marvels at his teaching.
Evening carries them out of the city.
Morning reveals the fig tree withered from the roots.
Peter remembers the word over the tree.
Jesus points to trust of the Divine.
Undivided heart-trust can move mountain into sea.
Prayer trusts the receiving before it is seen.
Standing in prayer requires release toward others.
A later manuscript repeats the release-warning.
Temple authorities confront him again.
They challenge his authority.
Jesus answers with a question.
John’s baptism becomes the test of their discernment.
Their reasoning exposes calculation instead of truth.
Fear of the crowd blocks their answer.
Refusal to answer leaves them unanswered.
